name: Mississippi

examFacts.notes: Administered by PSI for the Mississippi Real Estate Commission (MREC). The 80-question national portion and the 40-question state portion are scored separately, needing 70 percent and 75 percent respectively.

intro:
The Mississippi real estate salesperson exam is one of the tougher licensing tests in the Deep South. The Mississippi Real Estate Commission, known as MREC, licenses agents and contracts with PSI to deliver the test as 120 scored questions split into an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Mississippi state-law portion, with two and a half hours allowed for the national questions and ninety minutes for the state questions. The two portions are scored separately, and you need 70 percent on the national half and 75 percent on the Mississippi half, so a strong national score won't carry you if your grasp of Mississippi law is thin. That is why Mississippi-specific preparation matters as much as the national material.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. Mississippi tests its own rules in ways a national course never touches, from the Real Estate Brokers License Act that MREC enforces, to the Form A 'Working with a Real Estate Broker' disclosure a licensee must present at the earliest practicable opportunity, the five agency relationships Mississippi recognizes including disclosed dual agency and designated agency, the Property Condition Disclosure Statement required on sales of one to four residential units, and the broker trust-account rules that bar commingling. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Mississippi-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free Mississippi real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full Mississippi bank. Each tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real MREC practice test, and the ones you miss show you what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the Mississippi question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Mississippi real estate exam?
faq.a: The Mississippi Real Estate Salesperson Examination has 120 scored multiple-choice questions, split into an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Mississippi state-law portion. PSI delivers both portions in one computer-based session and mixes in five to ten unscored experimental questions that look just like the scored ones, so the time spent on those does not count against you.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Mississippi real estate exam?
faq.a: The two portions are scored separately. You need 70 percent on the national portion, which is 56 of 80, and 75 percent on the Mississippi portion, which is 30 of 40. If you pass one portion and fail the other you only retake the portion you failed, but both portions must be passed within the same two-month eligibility window after MREC approves your application.

faq.q: How much does the Mississippi real estate exam cost?
faq.a: PSI charges $75 to sit the salesperson exam, and that one fee covers both portions. A retake is also $75, whether you are re-sitting a single portion or both. After you pass, the salesperson license fee paid to MREC is $120. Registration fees are not refundable and expire one year after you register.

faq.q: How long is the Mississippi real estate exam?
faq.a: Four hours total. PSI allows two and a half hours for the 80-question national portion and ninety minutes for the 40-question Mississippi portion, taken back-to-back in one sitting. PSI runs a Mississippi test center in Jackson, with additional sites in Memphis, Mobile, and the New Orleans area, and your unofficial score report prints before you leave.

faq.q: Is the Mississippi real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It's a serious test, and the part that catches people off guard is the 40-question Mississippi portion. The national half covers the same principles taught in every state, but the state questions reward candidates who actually studied Mississippi's license law, the Form A agency disclosure, the five recognized agency relationships, and the Property Condition Disclosure Statement. Most people who fail leaned too heavily on national material and underprepared on Mississippi law. Honest reps on Mississippi-specific practice questions are the difference between a first-time pass and a $75 retake.

faq.q: What's on the Mississippi real estate exam?
faq.a: The 80-question national portion covers property ownership, land-use controls, valuation, financing, agency, property disclosures, contracts, transfer of title, the practice of real estate, property management, and real estate math, in the proportions set by PSI's content outline where contracts and agency carry the most weight. The 40-question Mississippi portion covers the powers and duties of MREC, licensing and license maintenance, property condition disclosures, agency disclosure and duties to the parties, out-of-state brokers and developers, trust accounts, broker supervision, records and documents, and advertising rules. Agency disclosure is the single largest state topic, so Form A, dual agency, and designated agency are worth extra attention.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Mississippi real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish Mississippi's 60-hour salesperson prelicense course, the highest-leverage move is grinding Mississippi-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. National principles you can pick up from any course, but the 40-question state portion rewards repetition on MREC rules, the Form A agency disclosure, and the Property Condition Disclosure Statement. The RealReady app gives you the full Mississippi question bank plus the national bank, detailed explanations, a missed-question drill mode, and per-category progress tracking so you can see exactly where you are weak.
